exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.description: A foundational press for building chest power, front delts, and triceps with a structure that is easy to track week after week.
exercises.cue: Pin the shoulder blades down, stay stacked through the wrists, and press in one clean line.
steps: Plant the feet hard and brace before the bar leaves the rack.
Lower to the mid-chest with control instead of bouncing.
Drive up through the bar while keeping the ribcage stable.
benefits: Builds a strong base for all pressing work.
Simple to progress with reps and load.
Fits both muscle-gain and strength-focused plans.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.description: One of the strongest lower-body builders for quads, glutes, and trunk stability under real load.
exercises.cue: Take a big breath, sit down and back, then drive the floor away on the way up.
steps: Set the bar securely and lock the upper back in first.
Break at the hips and knees together while keeping pressure through the whole foot.
Stand up with the chest proud and knees tracking cleanly.
benefits: Builds full lower-body force and control.
Improves strength carryover into other compound lifts.
Adds real density to heavy leg-day sessions.

exercises.name: Conventional Deadlift
exercises.description: A full-body pull that challenges the posterior chain, grip, and bracing quality from floor to lockout.
exercises.cue: Keep the bar close, wedge into position, and push the floor away instead of yanking with the low back.
steps: Stand with the bar over the mid-foot and set a firm grip.
Lift the chest and brace without letting the lower back round.
Finish by driving the hips through rather than leaning back.
benefits: Strengthens the back, glutes, and hamstrings together.
Improves bracing awareness under heavy load.
Carries over well to athletic power output.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.description: A practical shoulder-builder that adds pressing strength while giving each side a cleaner path to work through.
exercises.cue: Press just past ear height with a tall spine and zero lower-back overreach.
steps: Sit tall with ribs stacked over the hips.
Press in a smooth upward arc without clanking the weights together.
Lower under control so the shoulder stays loaded throughout the rep.
benefits: Builds shoulder strength with more freedom of motion.
Helps expose left-right strength gaps.
Works well in both hypertrophy and control phases.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
exercises.description: A classic bodyweight pull for building upper-back width, grip, and clean control over the full body.
exercises.cue: Start from a true hang and pull the elbows down instead of throwing the chin upward.
steps: Set the shoulders first before beginning the pull.
Drive the elbows toward the floor as the chest rises.
Return to a full hang slowly so each rep stays honest.
benefits: Builds lats, grip, and relative strength.
Improves body control in vertical pulling work.
Acts as a strong benchmark for upper-body performance.

exercises.name: Cable Crossover
exercises.description: A strong chest isolation movement for finishing with tension and shape without needing heavy loading.
exercises.cue: Move in a soft arc and keep a slight elbow bend the whole time.
steps: Lean slightly forward with the chest lifted.
Bring the handles together with a smooth squeeze out front.
Return slowly until the pecs feel stretched but still under control.
benefits: Improves chest connection near the end of a session.
Easy to scale for newer or advanced members.
Great for adding shape without joint-heavy loading.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
exercises.description: A sharp core drill for lower abs and pelvic control that quickly exposes swinging, weak bracing, or rushed reps.
exercises.cue: Lift the pelvis with the legs instead of only curling the knees upward.
steps: Start from a steady hang and kill the swing before the first rep.
Raise the knees or legs while curling the pelvis up.
Lower slowly and reset the trunk before the next rep.
benefits: Builds strong lower-ab control and trunk awareness.
Transfers well into athletic stability work.
Creates a serious challenge without extra equipment.

exercises.name: Triceps Dips
exercises.description: A useful pressing option for triceps strength with extra chest involvement and plenty of room to scale up or down.
exercises.cue: Keep the shoulders pulled back and avoid dropping deeper than your joints can control well.
steps: Set a stable grip with the chest open and elbows tracking back.
Lower only as far as you can stay strong through the shoulder.
Press back up cleanly with a smooth lockout.
benefits: Builds triceps strength and pressing endurance.
Works as either a main or finish-line movement.
Still effective with simple equipment at home or in the gym.
